heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Howard Hawks and Arthur Rosson. Written by Borden Chase and Charles Schnee.
clean: Directed by Howard Hawks and Arthur Rosson. Written by Borden Chase and Charles Schnee.

heading: Tom Dunson
raw: I signed nothing. If I had, I'd stay. You'll remember I joined your train after you left St. Louis.
clean: I signed nothing. If I had, I'd stay. You'll remember I joined your train after you left St. Louis.

heading: Tom Dunson
raw: I'm startin' my own herd. I've watched the land south of here since we left the Salt Fork. It's good land, good grass for beef, so I'm goin' South where it is.
clean: I'm startin' my own herd. I've watched the land south of here since we left the Salt Fork. It's good land, good grass for beef, so I'm goin' South where it is.

heading: Tom Dunson
raw: Wherever they go, they'll be on my land. My land. We're here and we're gonna stay here. Give me ten years and I'll have that brand on the gates of the greatest ranch in Texas.
clean: Wherever they go, they'll be on my land. My land. We're here and we're gonna stay here. Give me ten years and I'll have that brand on the gates of the greatest ranch in Texas.

heading: Tom Dunson
raw: [to Meeker] All right, we rounded up some of your stock and some of Diego's and some of everybody else's around here...I haven't got the time or the inclination to cut 'em out. So I'll drive 'em to Missouri and give you two dollars a head when I get back.
clean: All right, we rounded up some of your stock and some of Diego's and some of everybody else's around here...I haven't got the time or the inclination to cut 'em out. So I'll drive 'em to Missouri and give you two dollars a head when I get back.

heading: Tom Dunson
raw: [to Bunk] You started all this...we're three or four hundred head short and you killed Dan Latimer...stealin' sugar like a kid. Well, they whip kids to teach 'em better.
clean: You started all this...we're three or four hundred head short and you killed Dan Latimer...stealin' sugar like a kid. Well, they whip kids to teach 'em better.

heading: Tom Dunson
raw: All right. Anybody else? Say it now, 'cause I don't want ever to hear it again. I don't like quitters, especially when they're not good enough to finish what they start. Now go on! Speak up! Say it and you can join your friends here...
clean: All right. Anybody else? Say it now, 'cause I don't want ever to hear it again. I don't like quitters, especially when they're not good enough to finish what they start. Now go on! Speak up! Say it and you can join your friends here...

heading: Tom Dunson
raw: [to Matt] Go on, draw. I said 'DRAW'! [Long pause] Then I'll make ya. You're soft! Won't anything make a man out of ya?! [He seizes Matt's gun from his holster and throws it away] You once told me never to take your gun away from ya! [He hits Matt in the face several times] You yellow-bellied, chicken-livered...
clean: Go on, draw. I said 'DRAW'! Then I'll make ya. You're soft! Won't anything make a man out of ya?! You once told me never to take your gun away from ya! You yellow-bellied, chicken-livered...

heading: Nadine Groot
raw: If I was you, Colonel, I'd ponder on lettin' him be. He's a mighty set man when his mind's made up. Even you can't change him. Now he'll be headin' South. Mind he don't stomp on you on the way out.
clean: If I was you, Colonel, I'd ponder on lettin' him be. He's a mighty set man when his mind's made up. Even you can't change him. Now he'll be headin' South. Mind he don't stomp on you on the way out.

heading: Nadine Groot
raw: Colonel, me and Dunson...well, it's me and Dunson.
clean: Colonel, me and Dunson...well, it's me and Dunson.

heading: Nadine Groot
raw: Why do Indians always want to be burnin' up good wagons?
clean: Why do Indians always want to be burnin' up good wagons?

heading: Nadine Groot
raw: Never liked seein' strangers. This is 'cause no stranger ever good news'd me.
clean: Never liked seein' strangers. This is 'cause no stranger ever good news'd me.

heading: Nadine Groot
raw: [about Matt and Cherry] They was having some fun - peculiar kind of fun, sizing each other up for the future. Them two's gonna tangle for certain and when they do, it ain't gonna be pretty because they got a thousand miles to do it in.
clean: They was having some fun - peculiar kind of fun, sizing each other up for the future. Them two's gonna tangle for certain and when they do, it ain't gonna be pretty because they got a thousand miles to do it in.

heading: Nadine Groot
raw: [to Bunk] I could take the end of your nose off just as easy. A man in your age stealin' sugar!
clean: I could take the end of your nose off just as easy. A man in your age stealin' sugar!

heading: Nadine Groot
raw: [to Bunk] Your sweet tooth is almost as bad as having a whiskey tongue or liking a woman.
clean: Your sweet tooth is almost as bad as having a whiskey tongue or liking a woman.

heading: Nadine Groot
raw: It's all right. For fourteen years, I've been scared, but it's gonna be all right.
clean: It's all right. For fourteen years, I've been scared, but it's gonna be all right.

heading: Matt Garth
raw: It was all burnin', only Indians around, just all burnin' and smokin', smellin'. They were burnin' everything. I can see it. It's plain. I can see it. It was burnin' the wagons. People was screamin'.
clean: It was all burnin', only Indians around, just all burnin' and smokin', smellin'. They were burnin' everything. I can see it. It's plain. I can see it. It was burnin' the wagons. People was screamin'.

heading: Matt Garth
raw: I've been wondering too. The way he looked when we left him. It all happened so fast. I hadn't...I hadn't started out...I couldn't let him hang Teeler and Laredo...I don't know. He was wrong. I hope I'm right. I hope there's a railroad in Abilene.
clean: I've been wondering too. The way he looked when we left him. It all happened so fast. I hadn't...I hadn't started out...I couldn't let him hang Teeler and Laredo...I don't know. He was wrong. I hope I'm right. I hope there's a railroad in Abilene.

heading: Matt Garth
raw: Which would you rather have? What's behind, or what might be ahead?
clean: Which would you rather have? What's behind, or what might be ahead?

heading: Matt Garth
raw: How can a man sleep with Indians out ahead and him behind us?
clean: How can a man sleep with Indians out ahead and him behind us?

heading: Cherry Valance
raw: [to Matt] But your heart's soft. Too soft. It may get you hurt some day.
clean: But your heart's soft. Too soft. It may get you hurt some day.

heading: Tess Millay
raw: [to Matt] What are you so mad about? I asked you why you're angry. Is it because - because some of your men might get hurt, killed maybe?
clean: What are you so mad about? I asked you why you're angry. Is it because - because some of your men might get hurt, killed maybe?

heading: Tess Millay
raw: [to Matt] I asked you before why you were so mad. Is it, is it because your cattle, the cattle Cherry told me about, might run off? Or maybe, maybe you don't like the idea of helping, helping a bunch of women.
clean: I asked you before why you were so mad. Is it, is it because your cattle, the cattle Cherry told me about, might run off? Or maybe, maybe you don't like the idea of helping, helping a bunch of women.

heading: Early Tales of Texas (Journal)
raw: In the year 1851, Thomas Dunson accompanied by a friend, Nadine Groot, left St. Louis and joined a wagon train headed for California. Three weeks on the trail found them near the northern border of Texas. The land to the South looked good to...
clean: In the year 1851, Thomas Dunson accompanied by a friend, Nadine Groot, left St. Louis and joined a wagon train headed for California. Three weeks on the trail found them near the northern border of Texas. The land to the South looked good to...

heading: Early Tales of Texas (Journal)
raw: To Dunson it was just a job, a big job. Ever north they drove ten thousand cattle crawling through hot, dry country and by the end of the first two weeks they had covered over one hundred and sixty miles. Every mile had taken its toll --- quietly.
clean: To Dunson it was just a job, a big job. Ever north they drove ten thousand cattle crawling through hot, dry country and by the end of the first two weeks they had covered over one hundred and sixty miles. Every mile had taken its toll --- quietly.

heading: Early Tales of Texas (Journal)
raw: The days became longer, sleep was at a premium, hard work became harder and Dunson became a tyrant. After three weeks, they reached San Sabo. Here at last was water and a place to rest tired muscles and sore aching bones.
clean: The days became longer, sleep was at a premium, hard work became harder and Dunson became a tyrant. After three weeks, they reached San Sabo. Here at last was water and a place to rest tired muscles and sore aching bones.

heading: Early Tales of Texas (Journal)
raw: Thirty days on the trail and they reached the Brazos. The way now became harder. Hills and rocks impeded their progress. Each weary mile became endless. The men became morose and worried. The cattle restless and jumpy.
clean: Thirty days on the trail and they reached the Brazos. The way now became harder. Hills and rocks impeded their progress. Each weary mile became endless. The men became morose and worried. The cattle restless and jumpy.

heading: Early Tales of Texas (Journal)
raw: Sixty days, tired cattle and tired men. Trouble was not far off. The men sat in small groups, sullen and morose. The food became worse and Dunson was constantly on the alert for the first sign of mutiny. He felt as a man alone...
clean: Sixty days, tired cattle and tired men. Trouble was not far off. The men sat in small groups, sullen and morose. The food became worse and Dunson was constantly on the alert for the first sign of mutiny. He felt as a man alone...

heading: Early Tales of Texas (Journal)
raw: Secretly the rest of the men hoped Teeler, Laredo and Kelsey would succeed but not Dunson. He ordered the herd to move on and move they did with Dunson driving them at every step. The Red River was not far ahead and he meant to reach it by nightfall.
clean: Secretly the rest of the men hoped Teeler, Laredo and Kelsey would succeed but not Dunson. He ordered the herd to move on and move they did with Dunson driving them at every step. The Red River was not far ahead and he meant to reach it by nightfall.

heading: Early Tales of Texas (Journal)
raw: So Matthew Garth had the responsibility of a great herd - - and onward they went with the spectre of Dunson behind. He had promised revenge and Matt knew nothing in the world would stop him from fulfilling that promise. The time was coming...
clean: So Matthew Garth had the responsibility of a great herd - - and onward they went with the spectre of Dunson behind. He had promised revenge and Matt knew nothing in the world would stop him from fulfilling that promise. The time was coming...

heading: Early Tales of Texas (Journal)
raw: And that night they moved. The river was rising, they must get across while there was still time. In the meantime, Dunson had found men and ammunition and taken up the chase. He was determined to overtake Matt...
clean: And that night they moved. The river was rising, they must get across while there was still time. In the meantime, Dunson had found men and ammunition and taken up the chase. He was determined to overtake Matt...

heading: Early Tales of Texas (Journal)
raw: One hundred days and in Matthew Garth's heart a growing fear that there was no railroad...
clean: One hundred days and in Matthew Garth's heart a growing fear that there was no railroad...

heading: Early Tales of Texas (Journal)
raw: And history was written that day in Abilene, August 14, 1865, a day that marked completion of the first drive on the Chisholm Trail. Excitement and wild hilarity greeted the trail weary men and cattle as they poured into...
clean: And history was written that day in Abilene, August 14, 1865, a day that marked completion of the first drive on the Chisholm Trail. Excitement and wild hilarity greeted the trail weary men and cattle as they poured into...

heading: Others
raw: Title card: Among the annals of the great state of Texas may be found the story of the first drive on the famous Chisholm Trail. A story of one of the great cattle herds of the world, of a man and a boy - - Thomas Dunson and Matthew Garth, the story of the Red River D.
clean: Among the annals of the great state of Texas may be found the story of the first drive on the famous Chisholm Trail. A story of one of the great cattle herds of the world, of a man and a boy - - Thomas Dunson and Matthew Garth, the story of the Red River D.

heading: Others
raw: Simms Reeves: Plantin' and readin', plantin' and readin'. Fill a man full of lead and stick him in the ground and then read words at him. Why, when you killed a man, why try to read the Lord in as a partner on the job?
clean: Plantin' and readin', plantin' and readin'. Fill a man full of lead and stick him in the ground and then read words at him. Why, when you killed a man, why try to read the Lord in as a partner on the job?

heading: Others
raw: Mr. Melville: There's three times in a man's life when he has a right to yell at the moon: when he marries, when his children come, and when he finishes a job he had to be crazy to start.
clean: There's three times in a man's life when he has a right to yell at the moon: when he marries, when his children come, and when he finishes a job he had to be crazy to start.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Fen: I know you have work to do, Tom, but I want to be part of it. I love you. I want to be with you. Please take me with you. I'm strong. I can stand anything you can.
clean: I know you have work to do, Tom, but I want to be part of it. I love you. I want to be with you. Please take me with you. I'm strong. I can stand anything you can.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tom: It's too much for a woman.
clean: It's too much for a woman.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tom: I've made up my mind.
clean: I've made up my mind.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Fen: Oh change your mind, Tom. Just once in your life change your mind.
clean: Oh change your mind, Tom. Just once in your life change your mind.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tom: I'll send for ya. Will ya come?
clean: I'll send for ya. Will ya come?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Fen: Of course I'll come. But you're wrong.
clean: Of course I'll come. But you're wrong.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Fen: Go, please. If you're going to go, please go now. I want to be with you so much. My knees feel like...like they have knives in them. Bye.
clean: Go, please. If you're going to go, please go now. I want to be with you so much. My knees feel like...like they have knives in them. Bye.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Don't ever trust anybody until you know 'em.
clean: Don't ever trust anybody until you know 'em.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: I won't - after this. Thanks for telling me.
clean: I won't - after this. Thanks for telling me.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Yeah, well, it looks like we'll have to take you along. [he returns Matt's gun] Well, are you gonna use it?
clean: Yeah, well, it looks like we'll have to take you along. Well, are you gonna use it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: No, no. But don't ever try to take it away from me again.
clean: No, no. But don't ever try to take it away from me again.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: [to Groot] He'll do.
clean: He'll do.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: This is it. This is where we start growin' good beef...Everything a man could want. Good water and grass and plenty of it.
clean: This is it. This is where we start growin' good beef...Everything a man could want. Good water and grass and plenty of it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: Who does it belong to?
clean: Who does it belong to?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: To me! Some day, that'll all be covered with good beef. And I'll put a mark, a brand on 'em, to show they're mine too.
clean: To me! Some day, that'll all be covered with good beef. And I'll put a mark, a brand on 'em, to show they're mine too.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: What kind of a mark?
clean: What kind of a mark?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: I've been thinkin' about that. There'll be two lines, like this, like the banks of a river. It'll be the Red River brand.
clean: I've been thinkin' about that. There'll be two lines, like this, like the banks of a river. It'll be the Red River brand.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: I see a 'D' for Dunson, but my name's Matthew...I don't see any 'M' on that brand.
clean: I see a 'D' for Dunson, but my name's Matthew...I don't see any 'M' on that brand.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: I'll put an 'M' on it when you earn it.
clean: I'll put an 'M' on it when you earn it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: That's fair enough. I'll earn it.
clean: That's fair enough. I'll earn it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Tell Don Diego, tell him that all the land north of that river's mine. Tell him to stay off of it.
clean: Tell Don Diego, tell him that all the land north of that river's mine. Tell him to stay off of it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Fernandez: Oh, but the land is his.
clean: Oh, but the land is his.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Where did he get it?
clean: Where did he get it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Fernandez: Oh many years ago by grant and patent, inscribed by the King of all of Spain.
clean: Oh many years ago by grant and patent, inscribed by the King of all of Spain.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: You mean he took it away from whomever was here before. Indians maybe.
clean: You mean he took it away from whomever was here before. Indians maybe.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Fernandez: Maybe so.
clean: Maybe so.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Well, I'm takin' it away from him.
clean: Well, I'm takin' it away from him.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Fernandez: Others have thought as you, señor. Others have tried.
clean: Others have thought as you, señor. Others have tried.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: And you've always been good enough to stop 'em?
clean: And you've always been good enough to stop 'em?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Fernandez: Amigo, it is my work.
clean: Amigo, it is my work.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Pretty unhealthy job.
clean: Pretty unhealthy job.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: How'd you know when he was gonna draw?
clean: How'd you know when he was gonna draw?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: By watchin' his eyes. Remember that.
clean: By watchin' his eyes. Remember that.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: I will.
clean: I will.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: Missouri?
clean: Missouri?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Yeah.
clean: Yeah.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: That's what I figured.
clean: That's what I figured.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: And have you two been doin' a lot of figurin'? While you were at it, did you figure out the best way to get 'em there?
clean: And have you two been doin' a lot of figurin'? While you were at it, did you figure out the best way to get 'em there?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: Which trail to take? Yeah.
clean: Which trail to take? Yeah.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: He's afraid.
clean: He's afraid.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Groot: Afraid? Why you're crazy, you're looney.
clean: Afraid? Why you're crazy, you're looney.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: Am I?
clean: Am I?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: No, he knows that kind of fighting. What else?
clean: No, he knows that kind of fighting. What else?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Groot: And then come the war when you was away. He learned a lot of things for hisself. He learned that a ranch ain't only beef but it's money. But the war took all the money out of the South. He didn't know about money, Matt. He never had none. He didn't know what to do.
clean: And then come the war when you was away. He learned a lot of things for hisself. He learned that a ranch ain't only beef but it's money. But the war took all the money out of the South. He didn't know about money, Matt. He never had none. He didn't know what to do.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: You mean he just doesn't know who to fight.
clean: You mean he just doesn't know who to fight.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Groot: Yeah.
clean: Yeah.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: That's all right.
clean: That's all right.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Groot: He's just been waitin' for you to head the herd north and drive. A full drive that's never been done before. Nine, ten thousand head of cattle clear to Missouri.
clean: He's just been waitin' for you to head the herd north and drive. A full drive that's never been done before. Nine, ten thousand head of cattle clear to Missouri.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: We could make it.
clean: We could make it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Groot: We...well Matt, I'm glad you come home, cause, well, I'm glad you come home.
clean: We...well Matt, I'm glad you come home, cause, well, I'm glad you come home.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: I said 'Brand him.'
clean: I said 'Brand him.'

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: He's wearing a Meeker iron.
clean: He's wearing a Meeker iron.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: I can't see it.
clean: I can't see it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: [To Teeler] Brand him.
clean: Brand him.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Teeler: All right, but the next one up's another Diego.
clean: All right, but the next one up's another Diego.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: Brand him. Put the iron on all of 'em, Teeler. Anything you see, slap it with a 'Red River D' and burn it deep.
clean: Brand him. Put the iron on all of 'em, Teeler. Anything you see, slap it with a 'Red River D' and burn it deep.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Why not?
clean: Why not?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: You're gonna wind up branding every rump in the state of Texas except mine.
clean: You're gonna wind up branding every rump in the state of Texas except mine.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Hand me that iron, Teeler. You don't think I'd do it, do ya?
clean: Hand me that iron, Teeler. You don't think I'd do it, do ya?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: I don't.
clean: I don't.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Matt, I'm goin' to Missouri with every steer, cow, and bull that I can lay my hands on.
clean: Matt, I'm goin' to Missouri with every steer, cow, and bull that I can lay my hands on.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: I think Meeker might be real pleased to see our brand on his stock.
clean: I think Meeker might be real pleased to see our brand on his stock.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: That I'll argue with Meeker.
clean: That I'll argue with Meeker.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Cherry: That's a good looking gun you were about to use back there. Can I see it? [Matt hands his gun over] And you'd like to see mine. [Cherry hands it to Matt] Nice! Awful nice! You know, there are only two things more beautiful than a good gun: a Swiss watch or a woman from anywhere. You ever had a good Swiss watch?
clean: That's a good looking gun you were about to use back there. Can I see it? And you'd like to see mine. Nice! Awful nice! You know, there are only two things more beautiful than a good gun: a Swiss watch or a woman from anywhere. You ever had a good Swiss watch?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: Go ahead! Try it!
clean: Go ahead! Try it!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Cherry: Hey! That's very good!
clean: Hey! That's very good!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: Hey! Hey! That's good too! Go on! Keep it going!
clean: Hey! Hey! That's good too! Go on! Keep it going!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: There they are, Matt. Fourteen years of hard work. And they say we can't make the drive.
clean: There they are, Matt. Fourteen years of hard work. And they say we can't make the drive.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: They could be wrong.
clean: They could be wrong.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: They'd better be.
clean: They'd better be.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Cherry: Why drive 'em to Missouri? Why not turn west at the Red and head 'em for Kansas?...There's a railroad in Kansas, too...I saw the one in Topeka, and there's one in Abilene...
clean: Why drive 'em to Missouri? Why not turn west at the Red and head 'em for Kansas?...There's a railroad in Kansas, too...I saw the one in Topeka, and there's one in Abilene...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Buster McGee: Abilene, that's further west!
clean: Abilene, that's further west!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: Did you see it in Abilene?
clean: Did you see it in Abilene?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Cherry: I didn't get there. I met a girl in Kansas City. She fancied she could sing. She had...
clean: I didn't get there. I met a girl in Kansas City. She fancied she could sing. She had...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: You know, if we could head west at the Red...
clean: You know, if we could head west at the Red...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Groot: We'd save ourselves a heap of...
clean: We'd save ourselves a heap of...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: We're goin' to Missouri. I've seen buyers and cash in Sedalia. [To Cherry] What have you seen in Abilene?
clean: We're goin' to Missouri. I've seen buyers and cash in Sedalia. What have you seen in Abilene?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Cherry: Not a thing. Just the girl told me...
clean: Not a thing. Just the girl told me...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: We're goin' to Missouri. [Dunson walks off]
clean: We're goin' to Missouri.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Cherry: [To Matt about Dunson] I suppose if I tangled with him, I'd have to take you on too.
clean: I suppose if I tangled with him, I'd have to take you on too.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: You'll find him a handful by himself.
clean: You'll find him a handful by himself.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Groot: [about his false teeth, that he lost playing cards] If you was half-human, you'd give 'em back to me. You can see I could use 'em. Besides, it would help keep the dust out of my mouth.
clean: If you was half-human, you'd give 'em back to me. You can see I could use 'em. Besides, it would help keep the dust out of my mouth.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Quo: Keep mouth shut. Dust not get in.
clean: Keep mouth shut. Dust not get in.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Groot: Bet I ate ten pounds in the last sixteen days. Before this shenanigan is over, I'll probably eat enough land to incorporate in the Union. The state of Groot.
clean: Bet I ate ten pounds in the last sixteen days. Before this shenanigan is over, I'll probably eat enough land to incorporate in the Union. The state of Groot.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: [after shooting Bunk in the shoulder] You'd have shot him right between the eyes.
clean: You'd have shot him right between the eyes.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Just as sure as you're standin' there. Well, you shot him. You can take care of him.
clean: Just as sure as you're standin' there. Well, you shot him. You can take care of him.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Teeler: A man can't eat this kind of food. After we lost another grub wagon, we should have turned back.
clean: A man can't eat this kind of food. After we lost another grub wagon, we should have turned back.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Well, we didn't turn back and we're not goin' to. And even if we had, I couldn't replace what we lost. I'm broke. Got nothin' to buy it with. So you're on short rations and bad coffee. And you're gonna be until we finish the drive. And you're gonna finish it...Like it or not, that's it.
clean: Well, we didn't turn back and we're not goin' to. And even if we had, I couldn't replace what we lost. I'm broke. Got nothin' to buy it with. So you're on short rations and bad coffee. And you're gonna be until we finish the drive. And you're gonna finish it...Like it or not, that's it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: You didn't have to do that back there.
clean: You didn't have to do that back there.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: You joined in.
clean: You joined in.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Matt: Yeah, and I thought you were wrong.
clean: Yeah, and I thought you were wrong.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dunson: Well then why didn't ya...
